reviews.review_msg: Great material, but subjective marking. Two-part exams: first part - two terms you have to define. No rubric; you just have to pray that you guess what the professor wanted. Second part - essay. No feedback after grading, just an arbitrary rubric that, AGAIN, is released after the exam. If you're consodering to take it as an elective, please don't

Carman said she would regrade my exam essay and ended up completely ignoring my email afterwards. Now I have to do a whole grade appeal, when she could have easily sticked to her words. She wouldn't even round me up even tho I was 0.4 away so don't expect to get rounded up bc she has "no criteria on rounding".

Never taking her class again.

I have no idea how a 100 level course was graded as hard as it was ... I was genuinely confused. Of course I still got an A, but I had to work disproportionately hard for it in comparison to other 100/beginner level courses. 

Content was easy, but there was so much of it. Papers were marked disproportionately hard. Hm.

Course has multiple T.A.s. My T.A. nitpicked and took off marks despite my answer being the same as her feedback. Course was interesting at first, but got boring as it went on. Most, if not the entire course, was taken from a predecessor and was slightly messy. Only 6 assignments in total. Essays were difficult and instructions were vague.

Lots of reading to do and the first assignment was pretty difficult as the questions were pulled from different readings and it was not very specified. Good class overall I guess not the best but the prof was good.
Strange dude.

Nice and so funny ... but his class is not marked on par with other intro first year classes, and his TAs mark in-class assignment harshly and there isn't a lot of feedback. If you read the chapters and memorize the little details (because that one little idea WILL be on the exam!) you will be fine. Also, don't bother watching the videos.
assignments were hard for no reason, and there were lots of them

I took both 100+102 with prof Day. He was definitely one of the best profs I have had. He has lots of options to earn a good grade ( through quizzes , tests , and in class writes ) and everything is pulled straight from the textbook. You just have to spend 2 hours doing the reading a week at most, and you will be good :)

Day's recorded lectures are pretty dry but informative and get points across. But his in-person lectures are more hit or miss since he expects the heavy lifting to be done by his recorded ones. 

His tests, assignments and quizzes are challenging but mostly fair since they pull from the textbook.
excellent, logical teaching. Took 100 and 102 at the same time, got a C in 100 from a useless prof, and an A- in 102 from Day. Gotta be the teaching.

She seems like the sweetest lady! She's super nice and very interesting. She knows how to make lectures worthwhile (she brought in an awesome guest speaker) and although lectures are long, she does make them interesting. Be sure to study for iclicker tests and exams though!

This online class was a lot of work. Do not take it if you are looking for something easy.  She is really helpful if you need to clarify or ask questions. She's a great prof, very approachable and lovely. I was going through a hard time and you won't believe how comprehensive she is towards students. She definitely wants us so succeed.
Loved his enthusiam for 20 cent. Poetry GOOD JOB

Extremely brilliant prof as well as my easiest course this semester, lectures are really engaging and he is ridiculously passionate about what he is teaching.  Was pleasantly surprised with the profs at SFU as i wasn't expecting lecturers to be so excited about what they were teaching. Makes me want to take all of his classes
exasperating lectures--needs to be discriminating instead of encouraging moronic thought from all the eager beaver pseudo-intellectuals. unapproachable outside of class.
Very approachable and friendly;however,very disorganized when posting grades as well as posting lecture slides.

Great prof! Lecture notes usually put up the day before, would be better if put up sooner. Very helpful, willing to answer all questions. Fair marker, I don't know what the other guy was saying - we haven't had anything marked before this weeK!

The quintessential ol' history prof! He looks like one & def. acts like one: a little scattered, loves the topic, and not extremely forthcoming.  Go to his lectures, take good notes cuz his midterm's on them.  Takes attendance in tutorial but counts as participation marks. Overall, take him but 1st yr students: he doesn't hold your hand as others d
The professor is an excellent teacher. He is engaging, makes the material easily digestible, does not grade based on tests, and is extremely approachable.

THERE ARE NO TESTS! Mark is one of the kindest, funniest, and most caring profs I have had.  He truly does his best to engage students and make them consider real world applications of the subject matter.  If you love history, you will love Mark Leier.  If you don't love history, he will make it palatable.

Funny and approachable. Clear on concepts, not on assignment descriptions, so ask. (Seriously, a slight misinterpretation can result in the difference between an A+ and a B-.) If you have previous experience with tougher history courses, you can put in one day to do one of the assignments. If you're new to history, put in 2-3 days.
Do the questions
No Comments
Faran is a great prof. Though he might not be clear at times but you can always visit him during his office hours and he will make sure you understand the matter. Overall he is a good prof.

He is very clear in what he expects from you. Two essays and a midterm along with the final exam. One presentation for tutorial as well. Marking rubric is very clear, and if you do your studying the exams are fairly easy. He is always available for extra help. The textbook saved me for the two essays, I based my entire papers off the textbook.

Easier than BISC 101. Office hours don't help. Spends a lot of time on useless stuff during lectures. If she emphasizes something, it doesn't mean it'll be on the tests and vice versa. Overexplains and ends up complicating the topic, eventually confusing you so it's better to ask TAs for help. Lab exam is easier than 101 as it is open book.

the material may not be most exciting but a good prof should be able to make it interesting..it's not a completely hopeless subject.she really tried with her movies to make it interesting but I still found the class really dull and kinda pointless..I hate it when profs read off the PPT. don't even bother coming to review classes if you can read!

helpful in the lab and approachable but really unfair marking scheme.forget"answering the question"on the final just write everything you know that's remotely related to the question asked. I'd rather the exam was closed book but with a clear marking scheme.You really cant know how you did until you get it back..
Great public speaker, and is passionate about biology. She adds fun clicker questions for bonus points as well.
